reforms.description: An Innovation Agency has been established to support and promote innovation activities, particularly in small- and medium-sized enterprises, and aims to enhance the support for innovative activities and facilitate the development of new technologies. Additionally, the interest rate subsidy scheme for 'innovative' investment has been extended to cover a wider range of activities, promoting investment in new technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of the Innovation Agency and the extension of the subsidy scheme are designed to promote innovation and investment in new technologies, which are expected to enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been implemented as stated in the descriptions.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant but narrower changes aimed at enhancing innovation support without being structural or system-wide.
reforms.source_quote: an Innovation Agency has been set up recently, and a programme has been launched to promote the use of micro-electronics, in particular, in small- and medium-sized enterprises.

reforms.description: Special participation funds were established in 1982 to raise risk capital for innovations, primarily targeting small- and medium-sized enterprises, with attractive tax credits for investors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of participation funds aims to increase risk capital availability for innovative firms, which can lead to enhanced entrepreneurial activity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funds were established and are operational as described.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to support startups but does not constitute a major overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: Responding to this situation, special participation funds were established in 1982 in order to raise risk capital for innovations mainly for small- and medium-sized enterprises.

reforms.description: The Federal Government announced a subsidy scheme of Sch 1 billion from 1985 to 1987 to promote research in and application of micro-electronics.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The subsidy scheme is intended to promote research and application in micro-electronics, which is crucial for technological advancement and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The subsidy scheme has been announced and is in effect as stated.
reforms.importance_rationale: The subsidy represents a significant investment in a specific area of research but does not constitute a major reform of the overall funding landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The Federal Government announced a subsidy scheme (Sch 1 billion from 1985 to 1987) promoting research in and application of micro-electronics.
